finding: The circular user avatar ("N") overlaps and clips the "Sign out" button / sidebar bottom edge on every page in the product.
persona_rationale: A widget that overlaps a button on every single screen is the first thing I see and it tells me no one did a final layout pass.

suggested_fix.current: Floating avatar overlaps the sidebar footer / Sign out button with a clipping z-index
suggested_fix.proposed: Contain the avatar inside the 'Signed in as' row, or give the sidebar footer the higher stacking context so 'Sign out' is never occluded

finding: Three different colours are used for primary actions: blue (Publish changes, Create redirect, Create page), black (Apply settings, list Search button), and the inline Confirm publish is blue again.
persona_rationale: I genuinely can't tell which button is 'the' primary on a screen when the brand's commit colour changes per component.

suggested_fix.current: Two competing primary button tokens (blue and black) used across the same product
suggested_fix.proposed: Define one primary colour (blue) for all confirming actions; demote 'Apply settings' and 'Search' to the same blue, reserve black/neutral for secondary

finding: The same readiness state is rendered three contradictory ways simultaneously: a 'Blocked' pill (header), a 'Fix SEO title' chip, and 'draft · SEO Blocked' subtext — while the field below reads 'Leave blank to use page headline'.

evidence.quote: SEO title ... /placeholder: Leave blank to use page headline

persona_rationale: I fixed the wrong thing first because the chip, the pill, and the field helper all disagreed about what was wrong.

suggested_fix.current: One state shown with three different labels, plus a contradiction with the field's own optional helper text
suggested_fix.proposed: Single source of truth: header pill and chip share wording (e.g. 'Blocked — add a hero headline'), and never block on a field whose own helper text says it is optional

finding: After publishing, the yellow 'Confirm publish' card stays open ('This will replace the current live version…') and success is announced twice — a green toast in the top rail and a banner inside the panel.

evidence.quote: 'Changes published.' toast appears BUT the confirm card remains open asking to publish again

persona_rationale: A confirmation card that survives its own success, asking me to redo what I just did, is the kind of stale state I file bugs for.

suggested_fix.current: Stale confirm card persists after success and success is double-announced
suggested_fix.proposed: On success, dismiss the confirm card and show exactly one toast; the card must not invite re-publishing of an action that already completed

finding: The 'Builder outline' / 'Page blocks' card is dark-themed (near-black) and sits inside an otherwise entirely light admin shell.
persona_rationale: A single dark card in a light app looks like a component pasted from a different design file.

suggested_fix.current: One dark surface (Builder outline card) in an otherwise light design system
suggested_fix.proposed: Reskin the outline card to the light surface tokens used by every other panel, or commit to a documented dark 'structure' zone — not one orphaned card

finding: Block selection uses an asymmetric ring: a solid black border on the top/left and a thick cyan glow only on the right/bottom edges.
persona_rationale: A focus ring that is black on two sides and blue on two sides is unmistakably an unfinished hover/active state.

suggested_fix.current: Mismatched border (black) plus an offset glow (cyan) on a selected block
suggested_fix.proposed: Use a single uniform focus ring — one colour, even width on all four sides

finding: The hero CTA buttons ('STEP INSIDE', the orange CTA) carry a heavy hard-edged drop shadow / skeuomorphic offset, while the entire editor chrome uses flat pill buttons.
persona_rationale: The chrome and the canvas look like they were designed by two people with different shadow tokens.

suggested_fix.current: Skeuomorphic hard-shadow CTAs on the canvas vs flat chrome buttons
suggested_fix.proposed: Align the content CTA elevation with the flat system, or document the brand 'raised' button as an intentional content style

finding: Toolbar pill buttons (Pages, Blocks, Save draft changes, Live preview, Share, SEO) are all identical weight/colour, so the primary save and a passive 'Share' menu look the same.
persona_rationale: When every action in the toolbar has identical emphasis, there is no hierarchy and my eye has nowhere to land.

suggested_fix.current: All top-rail pills share identical neutral styling
suggested_fix.proposed: Give 'Save draft changes' (and/or 'Publish') a filled/primary treatment and keep navigation pills neutral

finding: Toasts have no consistent treatment: 'Changes published.' (top-rail green), 'Draft saved.' (panel-anchored green outline pill), and a separate in-panel 'Changes published.' banner all coexist.
persona_rationale: Three different success treatments in one tool means there is no toast system, just one-offs.

suggested_fix.current: Three distinct success-toast styles and anchor positions
suggested_fix.proposed: One toast component, one anchor (e.g. top-right), one style for success

finding: READINESS and STATUS columns are bare coloured dots (amber/red/blue/green) with no legend or text label.
persona_rationale: I can see four colours and have no idea what blue versus amber means without hovering — and colour-only is also an accessibility fail.

suggested_fix.current: Status encoded only as a coloured dot with no key
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a text label or tooltip per dot ('Needs work', 'Blocked', 'Published') and a legend; do not encode meaning in colour alone

finding: The scheduled-publish field shows 'Uses Pacific Time (America/Los_Angeles)' for an Australian (Vendingpreneurs) business.

evidence.quote: Label says 'Uses Pacific Time (America/Los_Angeles)' — for an Australian business.

persona_rationale: Scheduling a publish in the wrong hemisphere's timezone is the kind of locale detail that erodes my trust in the whole build.

suggested_fix.current: Hardcoded 'Uses Pacific Time (America/Los_Angeles)' label
suggested_fix.proposed: Display the business/admin timezone (e.g. Australia/Adelaide) or let the admin pick, and echo the chosen zone

finding: After saving a schedule, the field re-renders empty and no 'Scheduled for {date}' status appears anywhere — the success state is undesigned.

evidence.quote: field re-rendered EMPTY and no 'Scheduled for …' status appeared anywhere in the editor

persona_rationale: An action with no visible result is indistinguishable from a failure; I would assume the schedule didn't save.

suggested_fix.current: Schedule field clears with no confirmation after save
suggested_fix.proposed: After save, persist the value and show a 'Scheduled for {local date/time}' chip near the publish status

finding: Disclosure iconography is mixed: 'Builder support' sections use a right-triangle, the top-rail 'SEO' / 'Blocks' use a chevron, and 'Advanced SEO' uses yet another caret.

evidence.quote: Media assets / Approved claims and CTAs triangle disclosures

persona_rationale: Mixed disclosure icons in the same panel are exactly the kind of inconsistency I cannot unsee.

suggested_fix.current: Three disclosure glyphs (triangle, chevron, caret)
suggested_fix.proposed: Standardise on one chevron for all expand/collapse affordances

finding: The block 'settings' gear icon is grey on some blocks and solid blue on others depending on selection, inconsistently across states.
persona_rationale: The active-state colour jumping between blocks tells me the selected style isn't systematised.

suggested_fix.current: Gear accent colour tracks selection inconsistently
suggested_fix.proposed: Tie the accent colour to selection deterministically, or keep the gear neutral and accent only the card

finding: In the no-results state the primary 'Create page' is a full-width blue bar dwarfing the small adjacent 'Clear search' button — disproportionate button sizing.
persona_rationale: A primary button stretched to full width next to a compact secondary looks like a layout that never got spacing review.

suggested_fix.current: Oversized full-width primary next to a small secondary in the empty state
suggested_fix.proposed: Size the two empty-state buttons to a balanced pair (equal height, content-width)

finding: The redirects table sits in a small card at the top of an otherwise empty full-height canvas, and rows have no edit/delete controls, so the page reads as unfinished.

evidence.quote: Redirects table has no delete/edit controls — created redirects cannot be removed from this UI.

persona_rationale: Acres of empty canvas below a tiny table, with rows you can't act on, looks like a placeholder screen.

suggested_fix.current: Small top-anchored table on an empty canvas with actionless rows
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a per-row actions column (edit/delete) and let the table or an empty illustration fill the space intentionally

finding: The same toolbar button is labelled 'Save draft changes' in some states and 'Save draft' in others.
persona_rationale: A button that renames itself between states is a small thing, but small inconsistent things are my whole job.

suggested_fix.current: Inconsistent button label across states ('Save draft changes' vs 'Save draft')
suggested_fix.proposed: Pick one label ('Save draft') and keep it stable

finding: The 'Share' dropdown menu stays rendered open, overlapping the SEO panel/status pill, while other interactions (block actions, schedule) happen behind it.
persona_rationale: An open menu floating over unrelated controls is a clear missing dismiss-on-blur, and it hides the 'Blocked' pill behind it.

suggested_fix.current: Share popover does not dismiss on outside interaction
suggested_fix.proposed: Close the Share popover on outside click / when another menu opens

finding: The editor exposes a prohibited ARIA attribute and a non-top-level complementary landmark; the revision page has duplicate main landmarks.

evidence.quote: landmark-no-duplicate-main, landmark-main-is-top-level on the revision route

persona_rationale: Structural a11y violations are the invisible counterpart to visual sloppiness — same lack of a final pass.

suggested_fix.current: Invalid ARIA attribute and duplicate / mis-nested landmarks
suggested_fix.proposed: Remove the prohibited attribute and ensure a single top-level main per page

finding: A new page canvas renders entirely as light-grey placeholder text ('Hero headline', 'Hero body copy', 'CTA label') with no distinct 'unfilled' styling beyond low opacity, so it's unclear what is real content vs prompt.

evidence.quote: New page canvas is entirely placeholder ('Hero headline', 'Hero body copy', 'CTA label') with no guidance

persona_rationale: Faint grey text could be real copy at low contrast or a placeholder — an undesigned empty-block state.

suggested_fix.current: Placeholder text indistinguishable from faint real copy
suggested_fix.proposed: Give placeholders a clearly non-content treatment (dashed outline / 'Add headline' affordance) so unfilled blocks read as empty states

finding: A 'Rendering…' / 'Updating' status pill appears bottom-left over the canvas while the AI FAB sits bottom-right — two floating overlays competing for the same canvas corners with different shapes.
persona_rationale: Two differently shaped floating chips in opposite corners read as two teams' widgets sharing a canvas.

suggested_fix.current: Two unrelated floating overlays (status pill + AI FAB) in opposing corners
suggested_fix.proposed: Consolidate transient status into one consistent location/treatment distinct from the persistent AI FAB
